source.name: Swishy AI Motion Templates
source.license_notes: Use as inspiration and taxonomy only. Store short pattern summaries, not copied videos, template code, or proprietary assets.

layout_rules: Treat Swishy as a motion-pattern source, not as a static visual design system.
Extract reusable animation intent: timing, entrance direction, reveal type, bounce/spring feel, and hierarchy.
Keep implementation original and adapted to the product UI rather than copying template output.
accessibility_notes: Avoid motion that blocks reading or repeats endlessly without value.
Respect reduced-motion preferences for large reveals, parallax, and spring effects.
Do not use motion alone to communicate critical status.
implementation_hints: Use this source when a UI needs polish through motion: hero reveal, notification stack, typewriter text, animated metrics, or product demo sequences.
Translate templates into motion specs: duration, delay, easing, stagger, transform, opacity, blur, and spring behavior.
For app UI, prefer short 150-450ms microinteractions; reserve longer 800-1600ms sequences for hero or video-style presentation.

quality.notes: Derived from Swishy template listing and feature descriptions.

retrieval.ai_summary: Use Swishy as an optional motion-pattern reference for kinetic typography, notifications, website reveals, data viz motion, mockup animations, social cards, and launch/demo sequences. Store motion intent, not copied assets.
